conventions.name: Multipart Help Architecture
conventions.description: The help command outputs a concatenated command table from two sources: Part 1 (knowledge commands from K_TOOLS methodology/commands.md — always present) and Part 2 (project-specific commands from active project CLAUDE.md — varies per project). Commands are never duplicated — they are concatenated.

conventions.name: Longest-Match-First Command Routing
conventions.description: When matching user input to commands, the registry is sorted by key length descending. This ensures 'harvest --healthcheck' matches before 'harvest', and 'normalize --fix' matches before 'normalize'.

conventions.name: Command-to-Module Mapping
conventions.description: Every command belongs to exactly one K_* module. The command framework in K_TOOLS defines the registry and routing, but execution delegates to the owning module (K_DOCS for documentation commands, K_PROJECTS for project commands, K_VALIDATION for quality commands, etc.).
